context: Tests whether the agent implements the skill's humor post-mortem, blind spot detection, and recency-adapted clarification session — grounding questions in specific talk observations rather than asking generic questions, grading humor effectiveness, capturing spontaneous moments, and compressing questioning for older talks.

checklist.name: Per-joke grading
checklist.description: Each identified humor beat in the debrief report receives an effectiveness grade from a defined set (e.g., 'hit', 'nod', 'flat', or similar landing-quality labels) — not a numeric score or pass/fail

checklist.name: Joke questions grounded in observations
checklist.description: The debrief questions reference SPECIFIC humor beats from the analysis (verbatim quotes, slide numbers, or meme descriptions) — not generic 'did your jokes land?' questions

checklist.name: Meme slide questions
checklist.description: For meme-only or meme-with-text slides identified in the analysis, the questionnaire asks specifically whether the audience visibly reacted or whether the meme was talked over

checklist.name: Spontaneous humor capture
checklist.description: The questionnaire or tool includes a question or prompt about humor moments NOT captured in slides or transcript — improvised callbacks, audience riffs, heckler interactions, recovery humor from demo failures, or any jokes that happened in the moment. Can be a dedicated question or a section for unscripted moments

checklist.name: Promotion prompt
checklist.description: The tool or report includes the concept of promoting spontaneous humor to planned content — asking whether a joke that landed well should become a planned beat in future deliveries, or flagging well-received spontaneous material for future reuse. Can appear in the questionnaire, the report, or as a field in the output

checklist.name: Blind spot questions
checklist.description: The questionnaire asks about at least 2 things the analysis CANNOT observe: room energy, audience size, technical failures, physical stage moments, or audience reactions not captured in transcript

checklist.name: Demo reaction questions
checklist.description: For demo sections identified in the analysis, the questionnaire asks about audience engagement or reactions during the demo — whether the audience was engaged, reactions to demo outcomes, any failures or recovery moments. Can be a dedicated demo question or included within blind spot / audience reaction questions

checklist.name: Recency adaptation — recent
checklist.description: For the recent talk (within past week), the debrief contains detailed per-joke and per-interaction questions — at least 5 specific questions about individual humor beats

checklist.name: Recency adaptation — old
checklist.description: For the old talk (2+ years ago), the debrief is COMPRESSED — fewer questions, broader scope (e.g., 'any jokes you remember landing well or badly?' rather than per-joke grading). Demonstrably shorter than the recent talk debrief

checklist.name: Structured output format
checklist.description: The debrief report stores humor grades and blind spot observations in a structured format (JSON with named fields) — not just free-text prose
